content.problemStatement:
You are managing a garden with an NNN X NNN grid of flower pots. Each row and column of the grid is arranged in ascending order of flower heights. Your task is to find the kkk-th smallest flower height in the entire garden when all the heights are arranged in sorted order.

Note that the kkk-th smallest flower height refers to its position in the sorted order, not its uniqueness. Write a solution without taking any extra space.


Input Format

The first line contains two space separated integers NNN and kkk, representing the size of the garden and the value of kkk.
The next NNN lines each contain NNN space-separated integers, where each integer represents the height of the flower at that position in the garden grid.


Output Format

A single integer representing the kkk-th smallest flower height in the garden.
content.inputFormat: First line contains two space-separated integers N and k, representing the size of the garden and the value of k. The next N lines each contain N space-separated integers, where each integer represents the height of the flower at that position in the garden grid.
content.outputFormat: A single integer representing the k-th smallest flower height in the garden.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The flower heights in the garden are: [1, 5, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 13, 15]. The 8th smallest flower height is 13.
The flower heights in the garden are: [1, 3, 5, 7]. The 4th smallest flower height is 5.
The flower heights in the garden are: [10]. The 1st smallest flower height is 10.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using binary search. We start by finding the minimum and maximum possible values in the garden, which are the smallest and largest flower heights respectively. Then, we perform a binary search between these two values to find the k-th smallest flower height. In each iteration of the binary search, we count the number of flowers with heights less than or equal to the current mid value. If the count is greater than or equal to k, we update the right boundary of the search range to mid - 1. Otherwise, we update the left boundary to mid + 1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2 log M) per test case, where M is the maximum possible flower height. The binary search takes O(log M) time, and in each iteration, we count the number of flowers with heights less than or equal to the current mid value, which takes O(N^2) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the input and the variables used in the binary search.
